title: Extend Value/Type enums for M5 types
analysis:
Task 53 extends the foundational type vocabulary in reify-types for M5. The current Value enum has 6 variants (Bool, Int, Real, String, Scalar, Undef) and the Type enum has 5 variants (Bool, Int, Real, String, Scalar). We need to add collection types (List, Set, Map), Option, Enum to both; add Function type to Type; and introduce new definition structs (EnumDef, TraitDef, TraitMember, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, PortDirection).

Key challenges:
1. Value needs Ord for BTreeSet/BTreeMap usage. Currently has custom PartialEq but no PartialOrd/Ord. Must implement total ordering with f64 handling (use to_bits() for total order as already done in PartialEq).
2. Exhaustive match arms exist in value.rs (content_hash, PartialEq::eq) and ty.rs (Display::fmt) — all must be extended.
3. One external exhaustive match in reify-lsp/src/analysis.rs::format_value must also be updated.
4. New trait-related types (TraitDef, TraitMember, etc.) go in a new traits.rs module.
5. Content hash tags 0-5 are used for existing Value variants; new variants need tags 6-10.
6. Type's Display needs extension for new variants.
7. Hash derive on Type needs to be preserved (used for HashMap keys); new recursive Type variants (List, Set, Map, Option, Function) need Box wrapping for Hash to work.

Approach: TDD with small incremental steps. Start with Ord on Value (prerequisite for Set/Map), then add each Value variant group with tests, then Type variants, then trait definition types.

prerequisites.description: Create the new traits.rs module file and register it in lib.rs. This is a new file for EnumDef, TraitDef, TraitMember, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, PortDirection.

steps.description: Write failing tests for Value::Ord implementation: assert Undef < Bool(false) < Bool(true) < Int(0) < Real(0.0) < Scalar < String < Enum < List < Set < Map < Option. Test that values of the same type sort correctly within their type (e.g., Int(1) < Int(2), String("a") < String("b")). Test NaN and -0.0 ordering consistency. Place tests in value.rs #[cfg(test)] module.

Implement PartialOrd and Ord for Value. Assign type-tag discriminant ordering: Undef=0, Bool=1, Int=2, Real=3, Scalar=4, String=5, Enum=6, List=7, Set=8, Map=9, Option=10. For f64 comparisons, use to_bits() to get total order (consistent with existing PartialEq). For now, only handle existing 6 variants; new variants will be added as they're created. This makes existing tests pass.

Write failing tests for Value::Enum variant: construct Value::Enum { type_name: "Color".into(), variant: "Red".into() }, verify Debug output, test equality of same vs different enum values, test ordering (Enum sorts after String, alphabetical within), test content_hash determinism and distinctness.

Add Value::Enum { type_name: String, variant: String } variant. Update all exhaustive matches in value.rs: content_hash (tag 6, hash type_name then variant), PartialEq::eq (both fields equal), PartialOrd/Ord (by type_name then variant). No Display on Value currently, so nothing to update there.

Write failing tests for Value::List variant: construct Value::List(vec![Value::Int(1), Value::Int(2)]), test equality, test empty list, test nested lists, test ordering (lexicographic on elements), test content_hash determinism.

Add Value::List(Vec<Value>) variant. Update all exhaustive matches in value.rs: content_hash (tag 7, combine hashes of all elements with length prefix), PartialEq::eq (delegate to Vec PartialEq), PartialOrd/Ord (lexicographic).

Write failing tests for Value::Set variant: construct from BTreeSet<Value>, test insert/contains operations, test that ordering is maintained, test equality of sets with same elements, test content_hash. Requires Ord on Value to compile.

Add Value::Set(BTreeSet<Value>) variant. Add `use std::collections::BTreeSet` to value.rs. Update all exhaustive matches: content_hash (tag 8, sorted iteration over elements), PartialEq::eq (delegate), PartialOrd/Ord (lexicographic on sorted elements).
Write failing tests for Value::Map variant: construct from BTreeMap<Value, Value>, test insert/get, test equality, test content_hash determinism, test ordering.

Add Value::Map(BTreeMap<Value, Value>) variant. Add `use std::collections::BTreeMap` to value.rs. Update all exhaustive matches: content_hash (tag 9, iterate key-value pairs), PartialEq::eq (delegate), PartialOrd/Ord (lexicographic on entries).
Write failing tests for Value::Option variant: construct Some and None variants, test equality, test content_hash (Some(x) != None, Some(x) != Some(y)), test ordering (None < Some(anything)).

Add Value::Option(Option<Box<Value>>) variant. Update all exhaustive matches: content_hash (tag 10, None vs Some + inner hash), PartialEq::eq (delegate), PartialOrd/Ord (None < Some, then by inner value).

Write failing tests for Display on new Value variants: Enum shows "Color::Red", List shows "[1, 2, 3]", Set shows "{1, 2, 3}", Map shows "{\"a\": 1}", Option(None) shows "none", Option(Some(x)) shows "some(x)". Note: Value has no Display currently, but the task says 'update Display' — actually we need to add it or verify Debug. Check: LSP format_value is the display path. Test format_value for each new variant.
Update format_value in reify-lsp/src/analysis.rs to handle new Value variants: Enum, List, Set, Map, Option. This fixes the exhaustive match compilation error.

Write failing tests for new Type variants: construct Type::Enum("Color".into()), Type::List(Box::new(Type::Int)), Type::Set(Box::new(Type::String)), Type::Map(Box::new(Type::String), Box::new(Type::Real)), Type::Option(Box::new(Type::Int)), Type::Function { params: vec![Type::Int], return_type: Box::new(Type::Real) }. Test Display output: "Enum(Color)", "List<Int>", "Set<String>", "Map<String, Real>", "Option<Int>", "Function(Int) -> Real".

Add new Type variants: Enum(String), List(Box<Type>), Set(Box<Type>), Map(Box<Type>, Box<Type>), Option(Box<Type>), Function { params: Vec<Type>, return_type: Box<Type> }. Update Display::fmt exhaustive match with appropriate formatting for each. Update is_numeric to return false for all new variants (they're non-numeric).
Write failing tests for EnumDef: construct with name and variants, verify field access, test variant lookup helper. Write in tests/m5_types.rs.
Add EnumDef { name: String, variants: Vec<String> } struct in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq derives. Add a contains_variant(&str) -> bool helper method.
Write failing tests for PortDirection enum: construct In, Out, Bidi variants, verify Debug, Clone, PartialEq. Write in tests/m5_types.rs.
Add PortDirection enum { In, Out, Bidi } in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash derives.
Write failing tests for TraitRef and TraitBound: construct TraitRef with name and type_args, construct TraitBound wrapping a TraitRef. Verify field access and equality.
Add TraitRef { name: String, type_args: Vec<Type> } and TraitBound { trait_ref: TraitRef } structs in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq derives.
Write failing tests for TypeParam: construct with name, bounds, and optional default type. Verify field access.
Add TypeParam { name: String, bounds: Vec<TraitBound>, default: Option<Type> } struct in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq derives.
Write failing tests for TraitMember enum: construct each variant (Param, Port, Sub, Let, Constraint, AssociatedType), verify Debug and pattern matching.

Add TraitMember enum with variants: Param { name: String, ty: Type, default: Option<Value> }, Port { name: String, ty: Type, direction: PortDirection }, Sub { name: String, trait_ref: TraitRef }, Let { name: String, ty: Type, expr: String }, Constraint { expr: String }, AssociatedType { name: String, default: Option<Type> }. Derive Debug, Clone, PartialEq.
Write failing tests for TraitDef: construct a full trait definition with type_params, refinements, and members of various kinds. Verify field access and equality.
Add TraitDef { name: String, type_params: Vec<TypeParam>, refinements: Vec<String>, members: Vec<TraitMember> } struct in traits.rs with Debug, Clone, PartialEq derives.

Write failing integration test in tests/exports.rs verifying all new M5 types are exported from the crate root: Value::Enum, Value::List, Value::Set, Value::Map, Value::Option, Type::Enum, Type::List, Type::Set, Type::Map, Type::Option, Type::Function, EnumDef, TraitDef, TraitMember, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, PortDirection.

Update lib.rs to add `pub mod traits;` and re-export all new types: EnumDef, TraitDef, TraitMember, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, PortDirection from traits module. Ensure the integration export test passes.

design_decisions.decision: Use to_bits() for f64 total ordering in Value::Ord
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with existing PartialEq impl which already uses to_bits() for exact float comparison. This gives a total order where NaN has a defined position and -0.0 != +0.0 in ordering (matching the equality semantics).

design_decisions.decision: Type-tag discriminant ordering for Value::Ord: Undef < Bool < Int < Real < Scalar < String < Enum < List < Set < Map < Option
design_decisions.rationale: Task specifies this ordering explicitly. Undef sorts first (sentinel), then primitives in increasing complexity, then collections.

design_decisions.decision: Place trait-related types (EnumDef, TraitDef, etc.) in a new traits.rs module rather than adding to ty.rs or value.rs
design_decisions.rationale: These are definition-level types (not runtime values or type constructors), so they deserve their own module. Keeps ty.rs focused on the Type enum and value.rs focused on the Value enum. The traits.rs module will hold EnumDef, TraitDef, TraitMember, TraitRef, TraitBound, TypeParam, PortDirection.

design_decisions.decision: Content hash tags 6-10 for new Value variants
design_decisions.rationale: Existing variants use tags 0-5. Continue sequential: Enum=6, List=7, Set=8, Map=9, Option=10. Tags are domain-separated to avoid collisions.

design_decisions.decision: Use String (not compiled expr) for Let::expr and Constraint::expr in TraitMember
design_decisions.rationale: At the definition level, expressions are not yet compiled. TraitDef holds the syntactic structure; compilation happens later in the eval pipeline. Using String keeps reify-types dependency-free from evaluation concerns.

design_decisions.decision: Add PortDirection to TraitMember::Port instead of making it a separate Port struct
design_decisions.rationale: The task specifies PortDirection as a separate enum and Port as a TraitMember variant. Keeping direction as a field on the Port variant is the most natural representation and matches the language spec's port syntax.

design_decisions.decision: Update reify-lsp format_value for new Value variants
design_decisions.rationale: This is the only exhaustive match on Value outside reify-types. Must be updated to avoid compilation errors. This is a minor scope expansion but necessary for correctness.

design_decisions.decision: BTreeSet and BTreeMap from std::collections for Set and Map values
design_decisions.rationale: BTreeSet/BTreeMap require Ord on their element type, which we're implementing. They provide deterministic iteration order (important for content hashing) and are the natural Rust choice for ordered collections.

reuse.how: Use existing content hash infrastructure for new Value variants. Follow the tag-byte + combine pattern already used in value.rs::content_hash().
Reuse for hashing String fields in Enum type_name/variant, TraitDef name, etc.

reuse.what: Existing PartialEq pattern using to_bits() for float comparison
reuse.how: Extend the same pattern to PartialOrd/Ord implementation for consistent float ordering.
Already imported in value.rs and ty.rs; existing Scalar variants continue to use it unchanged.

reuse.what: Type enum (for TraitMember and TypeParam fields)
reuse.how: TraitMember::Param, Port, Let, AssociatedType, and TypeParam all reference Type for their type annotations.
Follow the same pattern of constructing each type to verify crate-root re-exports for M5 types.
Extend the existing exhaustive match with arms for new Value variants, following the same formatting conventions.
